# Fate System Toolkit — English (design reference)

The complete English text of the Fate System Toolkit SRD, included under CC-BY 3.0,
split into 9 chapters. Text only — no trademarked logos, fonts, or dice images.

**Positioning — this is NOT a playable rules system.** Unlike the Core/FAE sets in
`extras/`, the Toolkit is Evil Hat's official guide to *hacking* Fate: designing
custom magic systems, subsystems (vehicles, monsters, supers, mass combat…),
scale, wealth, and alternative rules. **Do not copy it into `game/reference/rules/`
as the active system.** The host AI consults it as a design reference when the
player wants setting-specific mechanics grafted onto Fate, or when a ruling has no
precedent in the active rulebook — cite it in `ruling` like any other source.

## Chapters

- [Introduction](01-introduction.md) — rules vs. rulings, the Bronze Rule
- [Aspects](02-aspects.md)
- [Skills](03-skills.md)
- [Stunts](04-stunts.md)
- [The Big Game](05-the-big-game.md)
- [Special Circumstances](06-special-circumstances.md)
- [Customized Tools](07-customized-tools.md) — consequences, zones, scale, wealth, weapons & armor
- [Magic](08-magic.md) — five example systems + building your own
- [Subsystems](09-subsystems.md) — kung fu, cyberware, monsters, squads, war, vehicles, supers, horror

No Traditional Chinese translation exists for the Toolkit (the faterpg fan
translation covers Core and FAE only); the host AI can still read it cross-language
for campaigns run in Chinese.

## License & attribution

**CC-BY 3.0.** Keep this attribution on redistribution:

> This work is based on Fate System Toolkit (found at http://www.faterpg.com), a product of Evil Hat Productions, LLC, developed, authored, and edited by Robert Donoghue, Brian Engard, Brennan Taylor, Mike Olson, Mark Diaz Truman, Fred Hicks, and Matthew Gandy, and licensed for our use under the Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported license.

> Fate™ and Fate Dice™ are trademarks of Evil Hat Productions, LLC.

Each chapter file carries a YAML frontmatter block (`source`／`license`／
`attribution`／`modified`) so RAG chunks built from it inherit provenance per
`protocol/RAG-PROTOCOL.md`. English text sourced from the CC-BY presentation at
fate-srd.com via the brunobord/fate-srd-markdown mirror. Full third-party notices:
[`../../THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.md`](../../THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.md).
